page: Microsoft Azure F32s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by 64.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The server includes 262 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks position the F32s_v2 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. Single-core stress-ng performance registers at 1415.4, while multi-core performance reaches 29827.67. The system demonstrates a strong Passmark memory score of 2783.23, with read-only cached memory bandwidth peaking at 1663.93 GB/sec for small blocks, dropping to 104.75 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX, AVX-512F, and AES instructions.

For specialized workloads, the server delivers average performance in software compilation with a Geekbench clang score of 22522.0 and ray tracing at 21894.0. Large language model inference is viable for smaller models, with text generation on a 135M model reaching a strong 300.39 tokens/sec, but performance degrades significantly on larger models, with a weak prompt processing speed of 5.62 tokens/sec on a 70B model. This highlights a clear tradeoff: the instance is highly capable for compute-heavy, integer, and vector-based tasks, but constrained by its 2.0 GB per core memory allocation for memory-intensive or massive model workloads.

The F32s_v2 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for batch processing, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations, as evidenced by its 11238.33 Passmark database score and 26.7 million Redis SET operations per second.
description: Microsoft Azure F32s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, alongside 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 29827.67 and a strong Passmark memory score of 2783.23. While it supports AVX-512 and AES instructions, its low memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound tasks. It is best suited for compute-heavy workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, designed for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32s_v2 is a compute-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 262 GB local storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory bandwidth for compute-heavy enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
64.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory performance with a Passmark memory score of 2783.23
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and medium database operations
